Title: Per-dataset filter from context menu + filtered-size preview
Notes: Arthur 2026-06-16. Tree-ds datasetProvider gets Filter… (✓ when set) → #dsFilterModal editing that dataset's .filter (createExprInput, filter mode). Worker 'count' mode (countAnalysis: makeRowSource + src.stream, one pass, counts total + filter survivors, surfaces filterErrors/calcolErrors) → count-complete {total,kept}. Apply sets ds.filter + re-analyzes (model=startAnalysis+currentFilter sync, cmp=runAuxAnalysis which reads ds.filter; syncs an open config-panel filter textarea). Dataset-generic. INSTANT estimate (Arthur's steer — model from the t-digests, NOT the file head): dsFilterSynthEstimate Monte-Carlos 4000 synthetic rows from each column's t-digest centroids + category counts (cumulative-weight arrays, binary-searched; reuses generateSyntheticRows sampling), runs the real compiled filter (+calcols). Sort-order UNBIASED + accurate for single-column predicates (smoke: Fe>50 → modeled 55% vs exact 53%, vs the biased file HEAD's 0%). CAVEAT surfaced honestly ('modeled — assumes columns vary independently, so multi-column filters may differ'): marginals lose JOINT correlation, so multi-column filters on correlated cols can be off. Head-sniff kept only as the not-yet-analyzed fallback (labeled non-representative). Reliable size = the exact one-pass count. Smoke experiments/filter-modal-smoke.js (modeled≈exact, exact proven against post-Apply rowCount). b1-differential 29/29 bit-identical. NEXT polish if wanted: auto-exact for small files.

Title: Copula-modeled filter estimate (OPTIONAL — multi-column accuracy)
Notes: Optional follow-on to A14 — improves the INSTANT filtered-size estimate for MULTI-COLUMN filters on correlated columns (today dsFilterSynthEstimate samples each column's marginal independently, so joint correlation is lost). Discussed Arthur+Claude 2026-06-16. Literal multivariate t-digest = NOT a thing (no canonical total order / quantile in >=2D). Realistic path = GAUSSIAN COPULA: keep the existing per-column t-digest marginals + accumulate ONE d×d correlation matrix in the SAME analysis streaming pass (Welford sum-of-products, no extra I/O); estimate-time = Cholesky → draw correlated normals → push through each column's t-digest inverse-CDF → run the real filter. COST: memory O(d²) trivial (d=30→~3.7KB vs digests' 10s-100s KB); CPU O(d²)/row added to the analysis hot path — free at d≤~20, ~seconds at d=100 over 1M rows (CAP via top-K cols or a d-threshold). CAPTURES monotone/linear dependence (good for grades); MISSES nonlinear/tail dependence; categoricals don't fit Pearson (one-hot blows up d → keep them marginal). RECOMMENDATION (Claude): do NOT bake into the default analyze pass — it taxes a hot path for ALL users to improve ONE preview on a SUBSET of filters, and the exact one-pass count is already truth-on-tap. Build only as OPT-IN / lazy (compute the matrix only when enabled or d below a cap), or skip entirely and rely on Exact count. Single-column predicates already accurate w/o it. Needs: corr matrix in worker complete msg + analysisFingerprint + serialize/cache plumbing.
